summary: InkDrop iteration 2 (SSE streaming + Nano Banana covers + chunk inline edit) tested end-to-end. Backend: 28/28 pytest passed including the 11 new iteration-2 tests. Frontend e2e on real preview URL: SSE generate stream overlay shows token-by-token prose, auto-navigation to reader after done, cover image generated by Nano Banana renders as 1.06MB base64 PNG within ~12s, regenerate button present, inline chunk edit (pencil -> textarea -> save) persists and re-renders, continue-stream tail renders live tokens (first byte ~12-25s), Library + Community show 3:4 cover thumbnails with vibe-gradient placeholders for not-yet-ready covers. All previously-fixed bugs still pass.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~840 lines mixing auth, story, streaming, cover gen, characters, voice, prompts. Strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth_router.py, story_router.py, stream_router.py, cover_router.py, characters_router.py) before adding more features.

_generate_cover_bg uses asyncio.create_task(...) inside SSE event_gen and from /story/generate. A FastAPI BackgroundTasks param (bg) is accepted in two endpoints but unused — switch to bg.add_task for consistency and proper lifecycle, or remove the unused dependency.

/story/{id}/cover/regenerate sets cover_b64 to None but the response from /cover then returns {cover_b64: null, cover_mime: <stale>, cover_status: 'pending'}; consider also clearing cover_mime on regen for cleanliness.
Streaming endpoints do not enforce per-user rate limiting. A single client can hold the SSE connection open and spawn parallel Claude streams. Consider a simple in-memory throttle per user_id.

Cover images are stored as base64 in MongoDB documents (1MB+ per story). At scale this will inflate the stories collection significantly. Consider GridFS or an object store (S3/blob) and store only URL/key in the story doc.
Chunk edit endpoint trims text but does not enforce a max length — could store arbitrarily large strings. Add Field(max_length=10000) to ChunkEditRequest.text.

stream_message is invoked without an explicit cancellation/abort if the client disconnects mid-stream. FastAPI does not auto-cancel async generators on disconnect — long Claude streams may keep running and bill EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. Consider Request.is_disconnected() polling inside event_gen().
test_credentials: test1@inkdrop.dev / pass1234 (pre-existing). Fresh signups during run: it2-<ts>@example.com / TestPass123!
seed_data_creation: Test users + multiple stories with generated covers persisted in DB; safe to ignore or purge. Library currently shows 3 stories for test1@inkdrop.dev (cozy_nostalgic with cover, thriller, poetic_slow_burn).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-2 flows verified. SSE first-byte latency on continue stream is 10-25s — any waits should be >=30s. Cover gen 8-20s typically. test_cover_regenerate_resets_to_pending_then_ready and test_cover_generates_within_timeout have pytest.skip() fallbacks if Nano Banana is slow/down. Frontend selectors used: [data-testid='stream-overlay'], [data-testid='stream-prose'], [data-testid='reader-cover'], [data-testid='reader-cover-img'], [data-testid='reader-cover-regen'], [data-testid='chunk-edit-{i}'], [data-testid='chunk-edit-textarea-{i}'], [data-testid='chunk-edit-save-{i}'], [data-testid='reader-streaming-tail'], [data-testid='reader-continue'], [data-testid^='library-story-'].
rca of the issue: No critical issues found in iteration 2. Initial transient 'streaming tail not visible in 10s' was a test-side too-short timeout (Claude first byte takes ~12-25s) — confirmed working with 35s wait. Not a product bug.
